answer:
\boxed{A}  

The study shows that serum pancreatic lipase has significantly higher diagnostic accuracy (AUC 0.948 vs. 0.906) and sensitivity (90.3% vs. 76.8%) compared to serum amylase, with only 2.9% of acute pancreatitis patients lacking elevated lipase versus 18.8% for amylase. These findings support replacing serum amylase with serum pancreatic lipase as the preferred biomarker for diagnosing acute pancreatitis.
